title: Promote a reusable component into the design system
description: Add a component to the design system as its OWN secondary entry point (`nx g @bespunky/nx-tools:ds-component button` -> packages/design-system/button/, imported as `@<scope>/design-system/button`). One entry point per component, so each is independently tree-shakeable, is its own boundary, and never fights over a shared barrel. THIS IS THE ONLY WAY A COMPONENT ENTERS THE DESIGN SYSTEM — hand-creating the folder yields a component that resolves in the editor and vanishes on publish, because the entry-point config IS the boundary. Composes the secondary-entrypoint generator, then applies the design-system specifics: the sass channel into the component's own SCSS (so `@use '<ds>/styles' as ds;` resolves under ng-packagr), a starting SCSS that reads TOKENS rather than inventing values, and an explicit public-API barrel (private members go in a `_`-prefixed folder named for what it holds — `_parts/`, `_material/` — and stay out of index.ts). RUN IT ON THE SECOND OCCURRENCE of a UI pattern — the first time a card lives in the feature that needed it; the second time you promote it, migrate BOTH sites, and delete the copies (see the bespunky-design-system:design-system-first skill).

name.x-prompt: What is the name of the component to promote into the design system?

library.description: Override the target library. Defaults to the workspace's design system (found by its `type:design-system` tag).
